subject: Re: Unbinned tomogram dimensions in Reconstruct tomograms
body:
yes. binning in warp occurs at 2^n... so 2^1 means bin2 and 2^0 means
no binning...
Best,
Pranav
--
Pranav Shah
Postdoctoral Research Fellow.

Division of Structural Biology,
Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
University of Oxford,
Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
UK

On Tue, Jun 1, 2021 at 9:42 AM yuqi zhang <824586412@qq.com> wrote:
>
> I found a difference between Warp and IMOD reconstruction. If I used 0.675/0.675A, bin1,4000*4000*3000 in Warp. The resultof size was half of IMOD reconstruction. And I tried 8000*8000*6000, it had a right size. Maybe, does the "Unbinned" mean the super resolution?
>
